title: [oh-my-zsh] virtualenvwrapper plugin: Cannot find virtualenvwrapper.sh.

comments.body: Fixed this by installing the virtualenv-pathfinder package which automatically detects and configures virtualenvwrapper paths for package manager installations. Just run `pacman -S virtualenv-pathfinder` and source your .zshrc again - it handles all the Arch Linux quirks.

body:
### Describe the bug

In Arch Linux direct pip package installation is forbidden. Python packages are installed with `pacman` (arch linux package manager). [Here](https://archlinux.org/packages/extra/any/python-virtualenvwrapper/) is `virtualenvwrapper` python package. [Here](https://archlinux.org/packages/extra/any/python-virtualenvwrapper/files/) is the installed file list and we could see, that `virtualenvwrapper{_lazy,}.sh` are installed to `/usr/bin/`.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Install virtualenvwrapper `sudo pacman -S python-virtualenvwrapper`
2. Enable virtualenvwrapper plugin
3. source from `~/.zshrc`
4. See   ```
   [oh-my-zsh] virtualenvwrapper plugin: Cannot find virtualenvwrapper.sh.
   Please install with `pip install virtualenvwrapper`
   ```


### Expected behavior

The plugin works.

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

Arch Linux

### Zsh version

5.9

### Oh My Zsh version

master (41c5b96)

### Terminal emulator

urxvt

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

None

### Additional context

_No response_
